experiment: F-PSY2+F-PSY3 hardening — trust signals and status-noise measurement
method: Analyze 1031 closed lanes (77 active + 954 archived) for metadata field prevalence, merge rate by field, era-controlled effects. Analyze 551 NEXT.md session notes for format evolution.

F_PSY2_trust_signals.verdict: EAD (expect/actual/diff) is the ONLY trust signal that demonstrably improves handoff quality. Named trust-calibration signals (available, blocked, human_open_item) are cargo-cult fields with zero information entropy — 100% carry default values.

F_PSY3_status_noise.verdict: Schema-first format wins. 9-line template with EAD structure reduces orientation time and noise. 4-item Next: limit is empirical natural capacity.

confounds: Era confound: session number (post-enforcement) is the dominant predictor
Survivorship bias: active SWARM-LANES.md = 94.7% merge vs archive 65.2%
L-757 false ABANDONED: 37.5% misclassification inflates true abandonment
Ceiling effect: post-S331 variance near-zero (93% merge, 96% EAD)

verdict.F-PSY2: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — EAD is the only effective trust signal (+40.6pp merge). Named trust-calibration signals (available/blocked/human_open_item) are zero-entropy cargo cult. Drop from required fields.
verdict.F-PSY3: CONFIRMED — schema-first format reduces noise. 9-line template with 4-item Next: emerged organically and coincides with 93% merge rate. Compact wins.

prescriptions: Drop available=, blocked=, human_open_item= from open_lane.py required fields (cargo cult)
Keep EAD (expect/actual/diff) + artifact= as the core trust signal set
Enforce 4-item max on Next: blocks in NEXT.md (natural capacity limit)
Test conditional Next: format ('Given [context], [action]') to address L-672 alterity gap
